response.justification: The candidate is a skilled full-stack software engineer with broad web, mobile, and embedded experience, but has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, GPU architecture, and deep neural network workload analysis — none of which appear in the candidate's background. Their closest adjacent experience is low-latency application development with DLang and some embedded/hardware tooling work (NI LabWindows, robotic arms), but these are far too removed from hardware performance architecture for AI chips to bridge the gap at a principal level.
strengths: Low-latency systems experience with DLang including async I/O, caching, and fibers — shows some systems-level thinking
History of working across the stack including embedded and hardware-adjacent contexts (robotic arms, NI LabWindows C/C++)
Long career with consistent progression and demonstrated ability to operate across domains
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the primary technical requirement of the role is entirely absent
No AI, deep learning, or neural network background; no GPU or SIMD/MIMD architecture exposure whatsoever
Holds only a BS degree, requiring 8+ years of relevant experience — but the experience is in web/full-stack development, not hardware performance engineering

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture expertise
